title: Cross-transport gateway — route an Event LoRa-island -> bridge -> ESP-NOW-island, dedup-once-across (DG-2 #16)
runner: hive; 4 tuxedo DFR1195 — D1=480e900e(xx:xx:xx) origin, D2=2cab5f69(xx:xx:xx) LoRa-router, D3=f91c8911(xx:xx:xx) BRIDGE(LoRa+ESP-NOW), D4=06ae082b(xx:xx:xx) ESP-NOW receiver; pilot-site TG provisioned by composer (prov2.py); my ssh for flash/run
hardware: DFR1195 (ESP32-S3 + SX1262); D3 dual-radio = SX1262 LoRa + onboard ESP-NOW(2.4GHz)
config: LoRa: wairoa_as923_nz SF7 916.8 -9dBm + 4B immediate-sender prepend + hive-MASK (D1->[D2] D2->[D1,D3] D3->[D2]) + ALOHA TX-jitter; ESP-NOW: ch1 broadcast + MAC-MASK (D3->[D4] D4->[D3]); per-transport TX channels (DATA_TX_LORA / DATA_TX) + mesh_broadcast (bridge pushes BOTH)
firmware_commit: r2-hive eed35f9 (bridge feature; this run as-flashed)

expectations.observed: D4 (ESP-NOW-only receiver) DELIVERED 12 distinct Events (dlv=11; msg_id 3,4,5,6,7,9,12,13,15,16,18,19) that ORIGINATED at D1 over LoRa -> the Event crossed LoRa->ESP-NOW at the D3 bridge. ~12/19 originated requests delivered (~63%).

D3 (bridge) logs directed_via from=f91c8911 next_hop=06ae082b x11 = it DIRECTS to D4 over ESP-NOW (the cross-transport egress, engine's transport-aware best_transport). ESP-NOW peer MAPPED hive=06ae082b. msg_id=3 flooded before the trail formed, then directed.

D3 DROP Duplicate x36 on its own re-broadcast echoes; D4 DELIVERED each msg_id ONCE. DG-2 dedup-once-across-transports holds (dedup keys on frame-carried origin+msg_id, transport-agnostic) — no loop, no double-deliver.

D1 hears D3 DIRECTLY on LoRa (LORA-RX sender=f91c8911 x48) but MASKS it (masked=true) -> traffic forced D1->D2->D3 via the hardcoded can_hear_hive; D1 accepts only D2 (sender=2cab5f69 masked=false x31). D2 RELAY x24.
D4's replies (reply_msg_id 32771+) retrace ESP-NOW -> D3 -> D2(LoRa) -> D1: D1 reply-DELIVERED x12. Full bidirectional cross-transport routing.

notes: TN-FR-2 = the site LoRa<->WiFi gateway (DG-2 #16), ESP-NOW-minimal variant (proves the cross-transport core fastest). The ENGINE AUTO-BRIDGES (core): D3's transport-aware neighbour table (D2 on LoRa, D4 on ESP-NOW) -> plan_forward picks egress per hop -> directed_via D4 over ESP-NOW, NO bridge routing code. Firmware = per-transport TX channels (DATA_TX_LORA vs DATA_TX) + mesh_broadcast (the bridge pushes BOTH carriers); dedup transport-agnostic on frame-carried origin. Delivery ~63% (vs FR-1's 11%) — the fast ESP-NOW leg avoids the LoRa half-duplex contention. NEXT: FR-2b = the TRUE LoRa<->WiFi/UDP gateway with PI5 (Linux r2-hive RECEIVER over real WiFi); FR-4 = role-based sensor/router/receiver pilot-site sim + the TN-FR-1-REL loose-jittered-HB two-arm. Baseline restored after (composer reattach-5).
